steps.title: Map the paragraph's moves
steps.content: The text first introduces Claude Shannon as a foundational figure in information theory. It then describes his 1948 paper and its use of the bit to measure information and transmission limits. Finally, Gallager's quote explains that Shannon's discovery helped launch the communications revolution.

steps.title: Reject narrower structures
steps.content: A overstates the brief parenthetical note that "binary digit" was shortened to "bit"; the text does not discuss common usage. C makes Bell Labs central even though it is only background. D focuses too narrowly on the paper and misses the opening emphasis on Shannon's importance. The correct structure introduces the researcher, describes part of his work, and shows its historical significance. The answer is B.
